title: relay.rs: DELIVER path acquires MLS lock, drops it, re-parses same payload -- double parse on has_group() check
description:
In daemon/relay.rs dispatch_notification(), the DELIVER handler (lines 195-246) does:

  1. Acquires mls lock, checks has_group(), parses payload as ClearMessage, then drops lock (lines 215-225)
  2. If has_group() AND payload parses as Chat -- drops the message
  3. Then on line 227, re-parses the same payload again: match serde_json::from_slice::<ClearMessage>(&payload)

The payload is deserialized twice from the same bytes. On the first parse (line 216), the result is checked with .ok().is_some_and() but the value is discarded. On the second parse (line 227), it is used.

This is a logic correctness issue in addition to the performance concern: if the DELIVER payload is a non-Chat ClearMessage (e.g. Profile, Payment), the first parse succeeds (returns Some non-Chat variant), has_group() is true, and the condition is_some_and(|m| matches!(m, ClearMessage::Chat{..})) is false, so the message is NOT dropped. Then the second parse re-parses it and hits the Ok(_) => 'ignoring non-chat' branch. This is correct behavior but the MLS lock was held while doing the check unnecessarily.

More importantly: the MLS lock is held across serde_json::from_slice on an arbitrarily large, relay-supplied payload. This was already filed for BROADCAST (nie-tb8e.7) but the DELIVER path has the same issue: mls.lock().await holds the mutex while parsing unvalidated relay data.
